task: address_in_network in utils.py does manual bit-twiddling with struct and socket. Modernise it to use the standard library ipaddress module.
known_issue: ipaddress.ip_network() defaults to strict=True and raises ValueError when the network string has host bits set (e.g. 192.168.1.1/24, a very common form in NO_PROXY settings). The old implementation masked the address correctly; the new one crashes proxy-bypass evaluation for those values.
expected_correct_fix: Pass strict=False: ipaddress.ip_address(ip) in ipaddress.ip_network(net, strict=False).
